마가복음 2:1.content: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.

마가복음 2:2.content: Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them.

마가복음 2:3.content: Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him.

마가복음 2:4.content: When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.

마가복음 2:5.content: Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”

마가복음 2:6.content: But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,

마가복음 2:7.content: “Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

마가복음 2:8.content: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?

마가복음 2:9.content: Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’

마가복음 2:10.content: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—

마가복음 2:11.content: “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”

마가복음 2:12.content: He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”

마가복음 2:13.content: He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.

마가복음 2:14.content: As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he arose and followed him.

마가복음 2:15.content: He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.

마가복음 2:16.content: The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”

마가복음 2:17.content: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

마가복음 2:18.content: John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”

마가복음 2:19.content: Jesus said to them, “Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast.

마가복음 2:20.content: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in that day.

마가복음 2:21.content: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made.

마가복음 2:22.content: No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.”

마가복음 2:23.content: He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain.

마가복음 2:24.content: The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”

마가복음 2:25.content: He said to them, “Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry—he, and those who were with him?

마가복음 2:26.content: How he entered into God’s house at the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?”

마가복음 2:27.content: He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

마가복음 2:28.content: Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”
